title: MosaicLeaks shows how research agents can leak secrets through web-query trails
friendlyTitle: AI research agents can leak secrets just by searching the web
summary: ServiceNow researchers introduced MosaicLeaks, a benchmark showing that deep-research agents can reveal private enterprise information through the sequence of external web queries they make. The work also reports that privacy-aware reinforcement learning can sharply reduce leakage while preserving task performance.
friendlySummary: MosaicLeaks shows that an AI agent’s web searches can reveal private information, even if no single search looks dangerous by itself.
whyItMatters: Businesses often focus on what an AI says, but the searches it performs can also expose intent and confidential facts. Teams using research agents on private documents should treat outbound queries as sensitive logs and route them through privacy controls.
friendlyWhyItMatters: If an AI works with private company files, its search history may need protection too. Privacy is not only about the final answer.
